summary: GuionViral iteration 5 — validated the NEW /api/generate-frames endpoint (fix for the reported bug 'Falla con los videos que subo de mi dispositivo'). Backend: created /app/backend/tests/test_generate_frames.py with 6 tests, budget-aware (exactly ONE Gemini call). Built a 6-second testsrc mp4 via imageio-ffmpeg, extracted 3 JPEG frames at scale=640 with fps=1/2, base64-encoded and posted to POST /api/generate-frames with tone/style/description/title. Response: 200, source_type='upload', source_url=None, source_title='TEST_frames_success', tone='viral', style='energetico y directo', frames_used==3, used_fallback=false, script_generado non-empty (>30 chars), thumbnail is a data:image/jpeg;base64,... URI, no _id leak. Persistence verified via GET /api/history/{id} (same id, same fields, no _id). Validation errors: POST with frames=[] -> 400 ('No se recibieron fotogramas del video.'); POST with frames=['@@@','###'] -> 400 ('Los fotogramas no son válidos.'). Light regression (no Gemini): GET /api/ -> 200 'online'; GET /api/history -> 200 list without _id; POST /api/files/tts (nova, short text) -> 200 MediaItem with kind='audio', size>500, url='/api/files/{id}'; GET /api/files/{id} -> 200 with Content-Disposition attachment, Content-Type audio/mpeg, Content-Length==size, MP3 signature (ID3/0xFF); cleanup delete OK. Result: 6/6 PASSED in ~15s. Frontend smoke (mobile 390x844): app loads without JS errors, title 'GUIONVIRAL' visible, tabs 'Generar' and 'Historial' present, onboarding modal shows 'Empezar' as expected, testIDs mode-link and mode-text present. Only a benign 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' console warning (pre-existing, non-blocking). No deprecated Expo packages / SafeAreaView issues detected in server.py or the tab screens.
action_items: (Info) Gallery mode key in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/index.tsx renders testID mode-<key>. Query for 'mode-gallery' returned missing — the key appears to be a Spanish spelling (e.g. 'galeria'). Confirm the exact key so future Playwright tests can select the Galería mode. Non-blocking.
(Carried over) Extend ScriptItem.source_type union in /app/frontend/src/api.ts to include 'upload'/'text' (already used by backend).

(Carried over, optional) In /app/frontend/src/utils/audioDownload/index.web.ts, drop link.target='_blank' so Playwright can capture the download event; real browsers already honor Content-Disposition attachment.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/generate-frames: validates empty frames -> 400 and invalid base64 -> 400 (after silently skipping bad entries). Correct ordering: it collects successfully decoded frames first, then raises 400 if none remained. Verified.
server.py /api/generate-frames: cleans up workdir in finally block (shutil.rmtree ignore_errors=True). No temp-file leak.
server.py /api/generate-frames: caps frames at MAX_FRAMES+2 (=8) as a safety net, matches the ~6 the client sends.
server.py /api/generate-frames: persists ScriptItem to db.scripts and returns it; find({}, {_id:0}) in /api/history keeps the mongo _id out of API responses.
server.py _generate_script_with_gemini: computes target_words from duration_seconds when provided, so the guion length matches the video — correctly wired from FramesRequest.duration_seconds.
Response model ScriptItem has source_type='upload' for both /generate-upload and /generate-frames, so history rendering treats device-frame runs identically to full-upload runs (good UX consistency).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_generate_frames.py (new: 6 tests — /api/generate-frames success + 2 validation errors + 3 regression checks)

success_rate.backend: 100% (6/6 in test_generate_frames.py). Full regression not re-ran to preserve Gemini budget per agent note.
success_rate.frontend: Smoke passed: app loads, no red screen, no JS errors, tabs Generar/Historial present.

seed_data_creation: One ScriptItem with title 'TEST_frames_success' created by the success test; session teardown auto-deletes items whose source_title/script_generado contains 'TEST_'. TTS media created for the regression test is deleted in-line.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The device-side frames fix works end-to-end. New tests at /app/backend/tests/test_generate_frames.py; JUnit at /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_generate_frames.xml. To reproduce the success path outside pytest: build /tmp/sample_frames.mp4 with imageio-ffmpeg (testsrc size=640x360 duration=6), extract 3 JPEGs at fps=1/2 scale=640, base64 them and POST to /api/generate-frames with {frames, duration_seconds:6, tone:'viral', style, description, title}. Preserve Gemini budget: ONE call per run. Frontend testIDs to select gallery mode still need confirmation (mode-<key> where key may be 'galeria').
rca_of_issue: Original bug: uploading a full mobile video to /api/generate-upload was fragile (large size / .mov HEVC / flaky mobile networks). Fix: on native, the client extracts ~6 thumbnails locally with expo-video-thumbnails and posts only small JPEGs (base64) to the new POST /api/generate-frames endpoint. Server decodes/writes each JPEG to a temp dir, skips invalid entries, requires at least one valid frame (else 400), calls Gemini with the same _generate_script_with_gemini pipeline used by the link/upload paths, then persists a ScriptItem with source_type='upload'. Verified: success returns a real script (>30 chars) with thumbnail data URI, frames_used matches, and history persistence works; both empty and invalid-base64 payloads correctly return 400. Web path unchanged (/api/generate-upload continues to accept the full file).
